fresh_facts.claim: President Trump extended the US-Iran ceasefire indefinitely on April 21, 2026.
The United States maintains a naval blockade of Iranian ports and the Strait of Hormuz.
Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps seized two cargo ships in the Strait of Hormuz on April 22, 2026.
According to IRGC Aerospace Force Commander Majid Mousavi, Iran's forces have recovered to nearly 70% of their pre-war strength as of April 19, 2026.
The IRGC released a video on April 19, 2026 showing underground missile and drone launchers in operation.
More than 10,000 US troops were reported heading toward the Middle East as of April 22, 2026.
The United States suspended dollar shipments to Iraq and froze security cooperation with its military as of April 22, 2026.
Nearly $500 million in cash deliveries to Iraq were blocked as of April 22, 2026.
Iranian General Esmail Qaani visited Baghdad to meet with political and militia leaders.
Peace talks in Islamabad collapsed between the US and Iran.
Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf declared that Iran will not reopen the Strait of Hormuz under US pressure.
Iranian officials refused further negotiations under current US pressure conditions.
Iran's leadership remains fractured over US peace talks, with divisions between Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf and IRGC hardliners.
IRGC commander Major General Ahmad Vahidi has direct access to Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei and opposes negotiations.
A Provisional Leadership Council consisting of President Pezeshkian, judiciary chief Mohseni-Eje'i, and cleric Arafi has been established following Supreme Leader Khamenei's killing.
An Israeli strike targeted the Assembly of Experts in Qom on March 3, delaying Iranian succession.
Iran is experiencing severe unrest suppressed by brutal crackdowns and internet blackouts according to ISW assessments.
Foreign militias, including 800-5,000 Iraqi Shia fighters, have been deployed within Iran according to ISW assessments.
Iranian security forces have experienced morale issues with reported arrests for refusals to fire on protesters.
US Senate Republicans rejected efforts to curb presidential Iran war powers in April 2026.

regime_fall.current_status: Iran faces a complex crisis with a ceasefire extension offering temporary diplomatic respite, yet underlying tensions remain critical due to continued US naval blockade, Iranian port seizures, military posturing, fractured leadership dynamics, and Iranian refusal to negotiate under pressure. The regime demonstrates both military recovery capabilities and diplomatic intransigence while confronting internal instability and economic pressures.
regime_fall.last_major_event: On April 21-23, 2026, President Trump extended the US-Iran ceasefire indefinitely while maintaining naval blockades, US troop deployments accelerated to the region, Iran seized cargo ships in the Strait of Hormuz, and Iranian leadership rejected further negotiations under current conditions, signaling entrenched positions despite temporary ceasefire extension.
